data.title: Amicus upgraded to Buy at Needham banking on kidney disease therapy
data.description:
Amicus Therapeutics (NASDAQ:FOLD) was upgraded to Buy from Hold at Needham with a $14 target, emphasizing that the company's kidney disease program, DMX-200, is undervalued.

The stock of the company is up about 5% today.

"Despite DMX-200 being a Phase III program, with US peak sales potential of >$1.5B, the opportunity in FSGS does not appear to be baked into FOLD's valuation," Needham analysts said.

In addition to the underappreciated value of DMX-200, Needham also sees upside in Amicus’s commercial Fabry disease drug, Galafold. 

Analysts at Needham said that despite being on the market for nearly a decade, Galafold continues to deliver double-digit yearly sales growth driven by rising global diagnosis rates for Fabry disease, which have nearly doubled since 2016.

data.title: David Tepper: Cutting beyond 50-75 bp could lead to ‘weaker dollar, more inflation’
data.description:
Billionaire investor David Tepper expressed a cautiously optimistic view on the Federal Reserve’s recent interest rate cut, suggesting that moderate easing won’t significantly impact the economy.

Tepper, who is the founder and president of Appaloosa Management and owner of the Carolina Panthers, believes the Fed could implement up to three rate cuts without major consequences, during an interview with CNBC.

“One easing, or two easings, or even three easings…just don’t matter.” He explained that even with these cuts, monetary policy would remain “a little restrictive” given current inflation levels.

He said that the market is already anticipating additional easing measures but warned against pushing rate cuts too aggressively.

Tepper described his current market outlook as mixed, noting that “I’m constructive because of the easing right now, but I’m also miserable because of the [valuation] levels.” He highlighted that stocks across the board are not cheap by historical standards, with the broader market trading at approximately 20x earnings.

On potential risks, Tepper cautioned that excessive rate cuts beyond the initial 50-75 basis points could lead to negative consequences, including “a weaker dollar, more inflation, and those sorts of things.”

He emphasized that the Federal Reserve should be careful about how far they push monetary easing to avoid unintended economic repercussions.

The investor also touched on tariffs, sharing that while he doesn’t generally favor them, he believes 10% tariffs are not significantly disruptive to the economy.

He suggested that some protective measures might be beneficial in an AI-driven world where employment patterns are changing, though he expressed opposition to higher tariff rates of 25% or 50%, which he characterized as “a little bit destructive.”

More on Interest Rates:

  • Federal Reserve lowers rate by 25 basis points; dot plot allows for more rate cuts
  • Reaction Roundup: SA analysts, experts weigh in on Fed’s 25 bp cut
  • Bank of America CEO says Fed needs to be mindful of the 'inflation picture'
  • Federal Reserve Should Save Cuts To Clean Up The Bubble Fallout
  • September 2025 Macro Outlook: Fiscal Flows, Bank Credit, And Fed Policy Implications

data.title: Exxon has 'no plans' to re-enter Russia, CEO Woods tells FT
data.description:
Exxon Mobil (NYSE:XOM) has no plans to return to working in Russia, CEO Darren Woods told the Financial Times on Thursday, despite efforts by the Russian and U.S. governments to link energy investments to a peace deal in Ukraine.

"We don't have any plans to re-enter Russia," Woods told FT in an interview, adding that Exxon (NYSE:XOM) executives are involved in talks with Russian officials about recouping $4.6B in assets expropriated by the country but not about investing there.

Woods said the talks began in early 2023, shortly after the company filed an arbitration case against the Russian government, which had seized control of Exxon’s (XOM) 30% stake in the huge Sakhalin-1 oil project.

Analysts say Russia is eager to attract western oil majors to provide investment and technical skills that are needed for complex offshore operations such as Sakhalin-1, but the western companies are hesitant to return to Russia after writing off billions of dollars following Russia's invasion of Ukraine.

Senior Exxon (XOM) executives reportedly held talks with Russian officials last month about returning to the Sakhalin-1 project as part of a wider push by the Trump administration to secure a peace deal in Ukraine, following President Putin's decision to amend the Russian decree that seized Exxon's (XOM) Sakhalin stake, potentially paving the way for the return of western oil firms.

data.title: PayPal-, Mastercard-backed Pine Labs targets up to $700M from IPO - report
data.description:
Pine Labs, a digital payments company in India, expects to raise as much as $700M from an initial public offering in the latter half of October, according to a media report.

The company, which is backed by U.S. payment giants PayPal Holdings (NASDAQ:PYPL) and Mastercard (NYSE:MA), has started roadshows for the planned offering, Bloomberg News reported, citing people familiar with the matter. It has reduced the expected size of the IPO from an earlier target of $1B after existing investors decided to offer few shares.

The IPO is taking place as the fintech space in India benefits from the government's push into digitization. Deliberations are still taking place and details, including the IPO size and timing, could change, Bloomberg said.

data.title: Microsoft signs deal to spend $4B on new Wisconsin data center
data.description:
Microsoft (NASDAQ:MSFT) said on Thursday that it had signed a deal to spend $4B on a new, second data center in Wisconsin.

The second data center — with the $4B to be spent over a period of three years — will follow Microsoft's previous $3.3B investment in the state, for a total of $7.3B. The first data center is housed in Mount Pleasant and is set to go online in early 2026, Microsoft said. 

“This datacenter is designed to help AI researchers and engineers build the world’s most advanced models, test ideas faster, and do it all more efficiently,” Microsoft Vice Chair and President Brad Smith wrote in a blog post. “It’s not just about running AI — it’s about creating it. This is where the next generation of AI will be trained, setting the stage for breakthroughs that will shape the future. New discoveries in medicine, science, and other critical fields will start right here, with the models we train in Wisconsin.”

The data center would be the “most advanced in the world,” with the Mount Pleasant facility containing “hundreds of thousands” of Nvidia (NVDA) GB200 GPUs and connected “by enough fiber to wrap the planet four times over,” Smith added.

The 66-year-old Smith also addressed the rising cost of electricity — which many partially attribute to artificial intelligence and data centers — and said Microsoft has worked to ensure the data center will not raise the cost for its neighbors.

“That’s why we’re pre-paying for the energy and electrical infrastructure that we’ll use — ensuring prices remain stable and protecting consumers from future cost increases because of our datacenter,” Smith wrote. “We will match every kilowatt hour we consume that comes from a fossil fuel source one for one with carbon-free energy we put back onto the grid. This includes a new 250 MW solar project in Portage County that is under construction to support this commitment. And our partnership with WE Energies ensures we will continually explore and add energy transmission, generation, and usage — under transparent tariffs that support grid reliability.”

Once it's fully operational, the facility is expected to employ around 500 full-time employees; and once the second data center is finished, that will rise to around 800, Smith added.

The announcement comes on the back of Microsoft's decision to invest $30B in the U.K. over the next four years, unveiled earlier this week.

data.title: FactSet Research stock slides after soft revenue guide, Q4 profit falls short
data.description:
FactSet Research Systems (NYSE:FDS) shares retreated 3.2% in Thursday morning trading after turning in fiscal Q4 earnings that missed the average analyst estimate, in addition to soft revenue guidance for fiscal 2026 

The financial digital platform expects FY26 GAAP revenue of $2,423M-$2,448M (midpoint $2,435.5M), vs. $2.45B consensus. Also for the year, organic annual subscription value ("ASV") is expected to grow 4%-6%.

Meanwhile, Q4 2025 adjusted EPS of $4.05, trailing the $4.13 consensus, fell from $4.27 in Q3 but gained 8.3% from a year earlier.

Revenue for the quarter ended Aug. 31, 2025, of $569.9M, vs. $593.4M expected, rose from $585.5M in the three months ended May 31, 2025, and $562.2M in the three months ended Aug. 31, 2024.

Organic ASV was $2,370.9M at Aug. 31, up $81.8M over the last three months and up $127.3M Y/Y.

Adjusted operating margin declined 200 basis points Y/Y to 33.8% in Q4, mainly due to lapping of the prior year's lower bonus accrual and higher technology expenses. That's also down from 36.8% in Q3.

Client count was 8,996 at Aug. 31, a net increase of 185 clients in the past three months, driven by corporate and wealth management clients. The count includes clients with ASV of $10,000 and more.

User count was 237,324 at the end of August, a net rise of 16,828 users in the past three months, reflecting an increase in wealth management users. The user count does not reflect the fiscal 2025 acquisitions.

Total operating expenses climbed to $419.6M in Q4 from $391.4M in Q3 and dipped from $434.3M in last year's Q4.

data.title: Google expands Cloud, AI infrastructure in Africa with goal to connect 500M by 2030
data.description:
Google (NASDAQ:GOOG)(NASDAQ:GOOGL) plans to expand its digital presence significantly in Africa with the development of four new strategic subsea cable connectivity hubs in the north, south, east and west regions of the continent, the tech giant announced today.

This is expected to provide Cloud access and artificial intelligence tools to 500M Africans by 2030.

"We're building vital connectivity across the continent, including the Google Cloud region in Johannesburg serving users across the continent, the Equiano cable running along the entire western seaboard of the continent, and Umoja, the first fiber optic route to directly connect Africa with Australia (running through Kenya, Uganda, Rwanda, Democratic Republic of the Congo, Zambia, Zimbabwe and South Africa)," said James Manyika, Google's senior vice president of Research, Labs, Technology and Society, in a blog post.

Google expects these investments to boost GDP in multiple nations, and provide AI training opportunities to Africa's youth. The continent's youth population is projected to double to more than 830M by 2050.

Google Translate added more than 30 African languages last year and plans to expand it to more than 50 next year.

"AI creates an unprecedented opportunity to benefit everyone, and Google is committed to making that a reality for people, businesses and communities across Africa," Manyika said.

data.title: Uranium Energy drops after short report from Spruce Point Capital
data.description:
• Uranium Energy Corp. (NYSE:UEC) fell 6.2% amid a short report from Spruce Point Capital.
  • Spruce Point disclosed it's short shares of Uranium Energy (NYSE:UEC). 
  • Uranium Energy didn't immediately respond to Seeking Alpha' email request for comment.

data.title: Global X taps into chips and quantum trends for its latest thematic ETF filing
data.description:
Global X, a leading thematic exchange traded fund provider with roughly $100B in assets under management, is preparing to launch a new fund targeting two cutting-edge areas of technology: semiconductors and quantum computing.

A recent filing with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission shows that the firm plans to introduce the Global X AI Semiconductor & Quantum ETF, which is expected to trade under the ticker symbol CHPX. The fund will be passively managed and come with a total annual expense ratio of 0.50%. 

CHPX is designed to track companies operating across the artificial intelligence semiconductor and quantum computing ecosystems. The ETF’s underlying index will be built around four sub-themes: 

AI Semiconductors: Firms developing GPUs, CPUs, ASICs, memory, networking chips, and other components essential for AI training and inference. 

Compute Systems Enablers: Companies providing AI-focused hardware and software systems, including servers, networking equipment, and next-generation data center solutions.

Data Center Infrastructure: Businesses delivering cooling systems, HVAC, and power management technologies to improve efficiency in AI data centers. 

Quantum Computing Technologies: Companies working on quantum systems that aim to solve problems beyond the reach of traditional computing. 

The launch underscores Global X’s ongoing push to expand its suite of thematic ETFs and tap investor demand for exposure to fast-growing technologies shaping the future of computing.

While no direct holdings were listed, its quite possible that CHPX will be filled with leading chip and quantum focused stocks suchas Nvidia (NVDA), Advanced Micro Devices (AMD), Intel (INTC), Quantum Computing (QUBT), D-Wave Quantum (QBTS), IIonQ (IONQ), and others.
